methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Classiq is a Series C quantum computing software company that has raised $95M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 peers across public and private markets: three publicly traded companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and eight private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $95M total raised proxy, yielding $162M. This multiplier reflects typical valuation expansion from Series C to later stages. The peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow quantum computing set—only one adjacent-stage peer met screening criteria, below the three-datapoint threshold for reliable triangulation. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable noise given the specialized nature of quantum technology subsegments.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $137M to $186M, centered at $162M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression midpoint. The primary uncertainty is the absence of tight peer comparables at Classiq's specific stage and technology tier; reliance on a single method limits confidence in precision. Additionally, quantum computing valuations remain volatile given the sector's nascent commercial stage and rapid competitive repositioning. Further clarity on revenue traction and customer concentration would materially refine this estimate.
